topics.name: Number and Numeration
topics.description: Covers fundamental concepts of numbers, their types, operations, and applications.

subtopics.name: Number Bases
objectives: Convert numbers from one base to another
Perform basic operations in different number bases
Apply number bases in computing contexts

subtopics.name: Modular Arithmetic
objectives: Define and apply modular arithmetic
Solve problems involving congruences

subtopics.name: Fractions, Decimals and Approximations
objectives: Perform operations on fractions and decimals
Approximate numbers to required significant figures and decimal places
Apply rounding in real-world contexts

subtopics.name: Indices and Logarithms
objectives: Apply laws of indices to simplify expressions
Define logarithm and apply laws of logarithms
Solve exponential equations using logarithms

subtopics.name: Surds
objectives: Simplify surds
Rationalise the denominator of surd expressions
Perform operations on surds

subtopics.name: Sequence and Series
objectives: Identify arithmetic and geometric progressions
Find the nth term and sum of AP and GP
Solve practical problems involving sequences

subtopics.name: Sets
objectives: Define sets and set notation
Perform union, intersection, and complement operations
Solve problems using Venn diagrams

subtopics.name: Logical Reasoning
objectives: Apply basic principles of logic
Construct truth tables
Use logical reasoning to solve problems

subtopics.name: Ratio, Proportion, Rates and Percentages
objectives: Solve problems on ratios and proportions
Calculate rates of change
Apply percentages in financial contexts

subtopics.name: Financial Arithmetic
objectives: Calculate simple and compound interest
Solve problems on hire purchase, discount, and profit/loss
Apply financial arithmetic to banking and investment

subtopics.name: Variation
objectives: Distinguish between direct, inverse, joint, and partial variation
Formulate and solve equations involving variation

topics.name: Algebraic Processes
topics.description: Covers algebraic expressions, equations, graphs, and inequalities.

subtopics.name: Algebraic Expressions
objectives: Simplify algebraic expressions
Expand and factorise expressions
Perform operations on algebraic fractions

subtopics.name: Linear Equations
objectives: Solve linear equations in one and two variables
Solve word problems involving linear equations
Change the subject of a formula

subtopics.name: Quadratic Equations
objectives: Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
Solve quadratic equations using the quadratic formula
Solve quadratic equations by completing the square
Interpret the roots of a quadratic equation

subtopics.name: Graphs of Linear and Quadratic Functions
objectives: Draw graphs of linear functions
Draw graphs of quadratic functions
Use graphs to solve equations and interpret solutions

subtopics.name: Linear Inequalities
objectives: Solve linear inequalities in one variable
Represent solutions on a number line
Solve and graph linear inequalities in two variables

subtopics.name: Algebraic Fractions
objectives: Simplify algebraic fractions
Perform operations on algebraic fractions
Solve equations involving algebraic fractions

subtopics.name: Functions and Relations
objectives: Define and identify functions and relations
Distinguish between one-to-one and many-to-one functions
Find composite and inverse functions

subtopics.name: Matrices and Determinants
objectives: Define matrices and perform basic operations
Find the determinant and inverse of a 2x2 matrix
Solve simultaneous equations using matrices

topics.name: Mensuration
topics.description: Covers measurement of lengths, areas, and volumes of plane and solid shapes.

subtopics.name: Lengths and Perimeters
objectives: Calculate perimeters of plane shapes
Calculate arc length of circles and sectors
Apply perimeter formulae to composite shapes

subtopics.name: Areas of Plane Shapes
objectives: Calculate areas of triangles, rectangles, parallelograms, and trapeziums
Calculate areas of circles and sectors
Find areas of composite shapes

subtopics.name: Surface Areas and Volumes
objectives: Calculate surface areas of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, and spheres
Calculate volumes of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres
Solve problems involving volume and surface area in context

topics.name: Plane Geometry
topics.description: Covers the properties of geometric shapes and their relationships.

subtopics.name: Angles and Lines
objectives: Identify types of angles and their properties
Apply angle properties of parallel lines
Solve problems involving angles at a point

subtopics.name: Triangles and Polygons
objectives: Apply the properties of triangles
Prove and apply congruence and similarity of triangles
Calculate the sum of interior and exterior angles of polygons

subtopics.name: Circles and Circle Theorems
objectives: State and apply circle theorems
Solve problems involving angles in circles
Apply tangent-radius and tangent-tangent theorems

subtopics.name: Construction
objectives: Construct triangles and quadrilaterals
Bisect angles and line segments
Construct perpendiculars and parallels

subtopics.name: Loci
objectives: Identify and describe loci of points
Construct loci satisfying given conditions
Solve problems involving loci and regions

topics.name: Coordinate Geometry
topics.description: Covers the geometry of lines and curves using coordinates.

subtopics.name: Coordinate Geometry of Straight Lines
objectives: Calculate the distance between two points
Find the midpoint of a line segment
Calculate the gradient of a line
Determine the equation of a straight line
Determine conditions for parallelism and perpendicularity

topics.name: Trigonometry
topics.description: Covers relationships between angles and sides of triangles.

subtopics.name: Trigonometric Ratios
objectives: Define sine, cosine, and tangent for angles in right triangles
Use trigonometric tables and calculators
Apply SOHCAHTOA to solve right triangles

subtopics.name: Angles of Elevation and Depression
objectives: Distinguish between angles of elevation and depression
Solve practical problems involving angles of elevation and depression

subtopics.name: Bearings
objectives: Express directions using compass bearings
Calculate distances and directions using bearings
Solve navigation problems using bearings

subtopics.name: Sine and Cosine Rules
objectives: State and apply the sine rule
State and apply the cosine rule
Solve triangles using sine and cosine rules

topics.name: Introductory Calculus
topics.description: Covers basic concepts of differentiation and integration.

subtopics.name: Differentiation
objectives: Find the derivative of polynomial functions
Apply differentiation to find gradients of curves
Determine turning points (maxima and minima) of functions
Apply differentiation to real-world rate-of-change problems

subtopics.name: Integration
objectives: Find the indefinite integral of polynomial functions
Evaluate definite integrals
Apply integration to find areas under curves

topics.name: Statistics and Probability
topics.description: Covers data collection, analysis, and probability.

subtopics.name: Data Presentation and Analysis
objectives: Collect, tabulate, and present data
Draw and interpret bar charts, pie charts, histograms, and frequency polygons
Calculate measures of central tendency: mean, median, mode
Calculate measures of spread: range, variance, standard deviation

subtopics.name: Cumulative Frequency
objectives: Draw cumulative frequency curves (ogives)
Use ogives to estimate median, quartiles, and percentiles
Calculate interquartile range from an ogive

subtopics.name: Probability
objectives: Define probability and sample space
Calculate probability of simple and compound events
Apply addition and multiplication rules of probability
Solve problems involving independent and mutually exclusive events

topics.name: Vectors and Transformations
topics.description: Covers vector algebra and geometric transformations.

subtopics.name: Vectors
objectives: Define vectors and scalars
Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors
Resolve vectors into components
Apply vectors to solve geometric problems

subtopics.name: Transformations
objectives: Identify and apply reflection, rotation, translation, and enlargement
Find images of points and shapes under transformations
Describe transformations using matrices
